Codification, which directed amendment of section 8206 of the “Agriculture Act of 2014”, was executed to this section, which is section 8206 of the Agricultural Act of 2014, to reflect the probable intent of Congress.Pub. L. 118–42, § 443Section was enacted as part of the Agricultural Act of 2014, and not as part of the Cooperative Forestry Assistance Act of 1978 which comprises this chapter.

children.label: A
children.text: on Federal land, non-Federal land, and land owned by an Indian tribe; and

children.label: B
children.text: by either the Secretary or a Governor or county, as applicable, pursuant to a good neighbor agreement.

children.label: A
children.text: the appropriate executive official of an affected county; or

children.label: B
children.text: in any case in which multiple counties are affected, the appropriate executive official of a compact of the affected counties.

children.label: i
children.text: National Forest System land; or

children.label: ii
children.text: public land (as defined in ); orsection 1702 of title 43

children.label: iii
children.text: National Park System land; or

children.label: iv
children.text: National Wildlife Refuge Land.

children.label: i
children.text: a component of the National Wilderness Preservation System;

children.label: ii
children.text: Federal land on which the removal of vegetation is prohibited or restricted by Act of Congress or Presidential proclamation (including the applicable implementation plan); or

children.label: iii
children.text: a wilderness study area.

children.heading: Forest, rangeland, and watershed restoration services
children.label: 4 Forest, rangeland, and watershed restoration services
i
children.text: activities to treat insect- and disease-infected trees;

children.label: ii
children.text: activities to reduce hazardous fuels; and

children.label: iii
children.text: any other activities to restore or improve forest, rangeland, and watershed health, including fish and wildlife habitat.

children.label: I
children.text: necessary to carry out authorized restoration services pursuant to a good neighbor agreement; and

children.label: aa
children.text: in a manner that is consistent with the applicable travel management plan; and

children.label: bb
children.text: not later than 3 years after the date on which the applicable authorized restoration services project is completed; or

children.label: ii
children.text: construction, alteration, repair or replacement of public buildings or works.

children.heading: Good neighbor agreement
children.label: 5 Good neighbor agreement
children.text: The term “good neighbor agreement” means a cooperative agreement or contract (including a sole source contract) entered into between the Secretary and a Governor or county, as applicable, to carry out authorized restoration services under this section.

children.heading: Governor
children.label: 6 Governor
children.text: The term “Governor” means the Governor or any other appropriate executive official of an affected State or Indian tribe or the Commonwealth of Puerto Rico.

children.heading: Indian tribe
children.label: 7 Indian tribe
children.text: The term “Indian tribe” has the meaning given the term in .section 5304 of title 25

children.heading: National Forest System road
children.label: 8 National Forest System road
children.text: The term “National Forest System road” has the meaning given the term in section 212.1 of title 36, Code of Federal Regulations (as in effect on ).March 23, 2018

children.heading: Road
children.label: 9 Road
children.text: The term “road” has the meaning given the term in section 212.1 of title 36, Code of Federal Regulations (as in effect on ).February 7, 2014

children.label: A
children.text: the Secretary of Agriculture, with respect to National Forest System land; and

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary of the Interior, with respect to Bureau of Land Management land.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The Secretary may enter into a good neighbor agreement with a Governor or county to carry out authorized restoration services in accordance with this section.

children.heading: Public availability
children.label: B Public availability
children.text: The Secretary shall make each good neighbor agreement available to the public.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: Subsections (d) and (g) of shall not apply to services performed under a good neighbor agreement.section 472a of this title

children.heading: Approval of silviculture prescriptions and marking guides
children.label: B Approval of silviculture prescriptions and marking guides
children.text: The Secretary shall provide or approve all silviculture prescriptions and marking guides to be applied on Federal land in all timber sale projects conducted under this section.

children.label: I
children.text: to carry out authorized restoration services on under the good neighbor agreement; and1So in original.1

children.label: aa
children.text: authorized restoration services under other good neighbor agreements; or

children.label: bb
children.text: authorized recreation services under the Good Neighbor Authority for Recreation Act.

children.heading: Termination of effectiveness
children.label: ii Termination of effectiveness
children.text: The authority provided under this subparagraph terminates effective .October 1, 2028

children.heading: Retention of NEPA responsibilities
children.label: 3 Retention of NEPA responsibilities
children.text: Any decision required to be made under the National Environmental Policy Act of 1969 ( et seq.) with respect to any authorized restoration services to be provided under this section on Federal land shall not be delegated to a Governor or county.42 U.S.C. 4321

children.heading: Receipts
children.label: 4 Receipts
children.text: Notwithstanding any other provision of law, any payment made by a county to the Secretary under a project conducted under a good neighbor agreement shall not be considered to be monies received from National Forest System, Bureau of Land Management, National Park System, or U.S. Fish and Wildlife Service land, as applicable.
